data.title: Bitcoin Price Stuck Below $90K as Traders Watch for a Breakout Toward $100K
data.description:
The price of Bitcoin is currently hovering below the $90,000 mark, leading many traders and investors to closely monitor the market for any signs of a potential breakout that could propel it toward the significant milestone of $100,000. With ongoing fluctuations and varying market conditions, traders remain vigilant, analyzing trends and indicators that might signal a shift in momentum, as the cryptocurrency community eagerly anticipates whether Bitcoin will manage to surpass this psychological barrier and continue its upward trajectory.
source: https://blocknews.com/bitcoin-price-stuck-below-90k-as-traders-watch-for-a-breakout-toward-100k/

data.title: Binance Wallet: The minimum participation requirement for the COLLECT TGE event is 240 points.
data.description: Binance Wallet has officially announced that the entry threshold for the 43rd TGE project, COLLECT, is 240 points.

data.title: Lighter's marketing manager: TGE and airdrops will take place within the year, with the airdrop accounting for 25% of the total supply.
data.description: Pilla.eth, Head of Marketing for the Lighter team, shared some details about the Lighter TGE during an AMA session today, focusing on the TGE, including: 1. The TGE and airdrop will take place in 2025, with the airdrop consisting of 25% of the total supply and having no lock-up restrictions. 2. The airdrop will not require claiming and will be directly deposited into the Lighter wallet of the token holder. 3. 50% of the token supply will be provided to the community, with more details on the token economics to be released gradually. There will be token buybacks, but the specific amount is yet to be determined. 4. The LIT token will initially only be tradable on the Lighter platform. Coinbase and Bybit have added LIT to their roadmaps, and the team will not pay for listing. 5. There will be no official Lighter NFTs, but some community NFTs (such as Fuego) are acknowledged, and the Meme coin airdrop is also a rumor. In addition, Pilla.eth revealed that Lighter is developing "a mobile application comparable to CEX applications."

data.title: Solana co-founder: Total stablecoin market size will exceed $1 trillion by 2026
data.description: On X, Solana co-founder Anatoly Yakovenko released his 2026 predictions, which include: 1. The total size of stablecoins will exceed $1 trillion; 2. Quantum computing (QC) and controlled nuclear fusion (Fusion) will remain as difficult to implement as they are today; 3. AI will solve a millennium-old problem; 4. 100,000 humanoid robots will be shipped; 5. Starship will complete two successful commercial flights.

data.title: Bitcoin Exchange Activity Slumps as Liquidity Thins Across Major Venues
data.description:
A new analysis highlights a sharp contraction in Bitcoin exchange activity, revealing a market increasingly constrained by thinning liquidity. The data combines exchange-level activity scores with recent reserve dynamics, showing that participation across centralized exchanges has dropped materially even as reserves sit near historical lows.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/bitcoin-exchange-activity-slumps-as-liquidity-thins-across-major-venues/

data.title: Japanese listed company KLab has launched a "dual gold financial strategy," allocating BTC and gold in a 6:4 ratio.
data.description: Japanese listed company KLab announced the official launch of its "Dual Gold Financial Strategy," beginning the phased purchase of Bitcoin and gold. The company plans to invest 3.6 billion yen (approximately $24 million) from its approximately 5.1 billion yen in financing, allocated at a BTC:Gold ratio of 6:4. As of December 25th, the company had purchased an additional 3.17 BTC, at an average purchase price of approximately 13.83 million yen per BTC (approximately $90,000 per BTC), bringing its total holdings to approximately 4.37 BTC. Simultaneously, it also purchased 1,860 shares of a gold ETF. (CoinPost)

data.title: Argentina passes budget setting fiscal path for Milei’s economic agenda
data.description:
The current President of Argentina, Javier Milei 2026 budget plan proposal received a green light from the South American country’s Senate. This decision marked a significant legislative victory that helped alleviate tensions that had arisen earlier between the libertarian leader and his close friend
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/argentina-passes-budget-setting-fiscal-path/

data.title: Trading platform Bit.com: Will systematically scale back its existing operations and launch a "user asset migration plan".
data.description: Cryptocurrency trading platform Bit.com has officially announced that due to business adjustments, it will be scaling back its existing operations and simultaneously launching a "User Asset Migration Plan." Bit.com will continue to release clear and complete user guidance through official announcements, emails, and in-app messages to assist users in completing the asset processing and migration process within a specified timeframe. Specific timelines, operational methods, and precautions will be subject to subsequent announcements and guidance. Public information shows that Bit.com was launched in 2020 and is a subsidiary/affiliated platform of Singapore-based cryptocurrency financial services platform Matrixport.

data.title: LayerZero Community Fails to Pass Protocol Fee Activation Vote
data.description: According to Foresight News, the LayerZero community's vote on whether to activate the protocol fee mechanism did not pass due to insufficient quorum. The next vote is scheduled to take place in six months. The proposal included a decision on whether to activate the LayerZero protocol fee mechanism, which would impose a fee on each LayerZero message not exceeding the cost of verification and execution. The collected fees would be converted into ZRO and subsequently destroyed.

data.title: JPMorgan freezes stablecoin startups Blindpay and Kontigo accounts over Venezuela sanctions
data.description:
JPMorgan has frozen the accounts of two Y Combinator-backed stablecoin startups Blindpay and Kontigo over links to Venezuela, a country currently under heavy U. S. sanctions. According to The Information, both startups had connected to JPMorgan through Checkbook, a U. S. -based payments company. But the association with high-risk jurisdictions set off alarm bells inside the bank. JPMorgan […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/jpmorgan-freezes-stablecoin-startup-accounts/

data.title: Planet Midday News
data.description:
1. Lighter: The final batch of points for Season 2 has been distributed; Sorceress Points have been removed and redistributed.
2. LayerZero's third "fee switch" vote failed to reach the quorum requirement and will remain closed.
3. JPMorgan Chase has frozen multiple stablecoin startup accounts, involving high-risk markets such as Venezuela.
4. Yuga Labs announced the acquisition of Improbable, the Unreal Engine creation platform, and will absorb its development team.
5. An address suspected of being associated with the YGG vault deposited 40 million YGG, worth $2.65 million, into Binance.
6. Coinbase CEO: The Genius Act will not be amended; this is Coinbase's red line.
7. Galaxy Research Director: US ETPs are performing steadily; it's only a matter of time before Bitcoin follows gold as a hedge against currency devaluation.
8. Trader pension-usdt.eth turned short after making over $25 million in profits.
9. CryptoQuant Analyst: ETH contract trading volume has reached a new record this year.
